page: Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-intel is a virtual machine instance in the High Performance family, featuring 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores on an Intel Xeon Cascadelake architecture running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, meaning compute resources are multiplexed. It includes 12.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB per core. Storage is bundled with 260 GB of local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate mixed performance profiles. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and gzip compression single-threaded tests scoring in the average range. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is constrained, as evidenced by weak ratings in stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory performance shows average cached read bandwidth of 421.33 GB/sec, but uncached large-block reads drop to a weak 36.96 GB/sec. Database operations and static web serving throughput also fall into the lower performance tier.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. The inclusion of 260 GB of fast NVMe storage provides high resource density for storage-sensitive but compute-light applications. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, basic Redis caching, and general-purpose utility workloads where budget efficiency is prioritized over high-throughput multi-threaded compute.
description: The Vultr vhp-4c-12gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server featuring 4 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon Cascadelake architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 12.0 GB of RAM and 260 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU and cached memory read performance, but multi-core and uncached memory operations are relatively weak. This shared-CPU resource profile offers a budget-friendly option for workloads requiring moderate memory and fast local storage without sustained high-compute demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, development environments, Redis caching, and basic utility tasks.
